question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that consistently returns HTTP 404 from the mirror node REST API, causing the retrySpec in clientSubscribe() to exhaust all maxAttempts retries per publish event, thereby multiplying the number of outbound HTTP requests to the mirror node by (maxAttempts+1) and increasing its CPU and I/O resource consumption by over 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a sustained stream of 404 responses from the mirror node by submitting transactions that are never indexed, causing the retryWhen(retrySpec) chain in clientSubscribe() to repeatedly re-query /transactions/{transactionId} up to maxAttempts times per event, amplifying mirror node query load by a factor proportional to retry depth and exceeding 30% resource increase without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unbounded flatMap() operator in clientSubscribe() — which has no concurrency cap — to cause the monitor to issue an unbounded number of concurrent HTTP GET /transactions/{transactionId} requests to the mirror node simultaneously, exhausting its thread pool and connection resources and raising processing node resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a high volume of PublishResponse events to be emitted via onPublish(), each passing the shouldSample() check at samplePercent=1.0 (the default), so that every event triggers a REST API call to the mirror node via clientSubscribe(), steadily increasing the mirror node's inbound HTTP request rate and resource consumption by over 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: shouldSample()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default samplePercent=1.0 in RestSubscriberProperties — meaning ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextDouble() < 1.0 is always true — to ensure every single publish event results in a REST API call to the mirror node, maximizing query throughput and driving mirror node resource consumption above 30% of baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user, by influencing the configuration to set a high subscribers count (up to the @Max(1024) limit in AbstractSubscriberProperties), cause createSubscriptions() to instantiate 1024 RestSubscription objects each with their own Sinks.Many sink, so that every onPublish() event fans out to 1024 concurrent REST API calls to the mirror node, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout() value in clientSubscribe() — which defaults to 5 seconds — to keep HTTP connections to the mirror node open for the full timeout duration by causing the mirror node to delay responses (e.g., via slow transaction indexing), thereby holding open large numbers of concurrent connections and increasing mirror node resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the retrySpec's minBackoff and maxBackoff to be configured at very low values (e.g., nanoseconds as seen in tests), so that retry attempts in clientSubscribe() fire in rapid succession against the mirror node's /transactions/{transactionId} endpoint, generating a burst of repeated queries that increases mirror node CPU and network I/O by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction response from the mirror node that contains an extremely large transactions list, causing Iterables.getFirst(response.getTransactions(), null) in toResponse() to process a large collection object, increasing heap allocation and GC pressure on the monitor JVM which in turn increases the rate of REST API polling to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to return a malformed consensusTimestamp string (e.g., one with many dot-separated parts) that causes StringUtils.split(timestamp, '.') in toResponse() to produce an unexpected array, triggering repeated error paths and additional retry-driven REST calls to the mirror node, increasing its resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publishOn(Schedulers.parallel()) call in clientSubscribe() to cause the monitor to schedule a large number of parallel REST API tasks on the shared parallel scheduler, saturating the scheduler's thread pool and causing cascading delays in mirror node request processing that increase its resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() pattern in the subscriptions field — where the cache is shared across all callers — to cause a scenario where a large number of subscriptions are cached and continuously active, each independently polling the mirror node REST API and collectively increasing its resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a concurrency limit in the flatMap() call in clientSubscribe() — unlike flatMap(f, concurrency) — to cause an unbounded number of in-flight HTTP requests to the mirror node's /transactions/{transactionId} endpoint, exhausting the mirror node's HTTP connection pool and increasing its resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where the mirror node consistently returns HTTP 404 for submitted transaction IDs — because the transactions are submitted but never reach consensus — causing shouldRetry() to return true on every attempt and the retrySpec to fire maxAttempts additional requests per event, amplifying mirror node load by a factor of (maxAttempts+1) and exceeding 30% resource increase?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user, under preconditions where multiple RestSubscriberProperties entries exist in subscribeProperties.getRest(), cause createSubscriptions() to create a large number of RestSubscription instances (one per subscriber per scenario), each independently consuming from the same onPublish() event stream and each issuing separate REST API calls to the mirror node, multiplying its resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that onPublish() calls subscriptions.filter(s -> shouldSample(s, response)).map(RestSubscription::getSink).subscribe(s -> s.tryEmitNext(response)) synchronously for every publish event, causing a fan-out to all active sinks simultaneously and triggering parallel REST API calls to the mirror node that collectively increase its resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the take(properties.getLimit(), true) combined with getLimit() returning Long.MAX_VALUE when limit <= 0 in RestSubscriberProperties, causing clientSubscribe() to run indefinitely without a natural termination, continuously issuing REST API calls to the mirror node and sustaining resource consumption above 30% for an extended period?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to return slow HTTP responses (e.g., by exploiting slow transaction indexing), so that the timeout(properties.getTimeout()) in clientSubscribe() fires repeatedly, each timeout triggering a TimeoutException that is caught by onErrorResume, and the monitor immediately re-subscribes and re-queries, generating a continuous stream of timed-out requests that increase mirror node resource consumption above 30%?
